q:
Which one is non-polarized capacitors?
A. supercapacitors,
B. electrolytic capacitors
C. film capacitors 2024

A _______ is an electrical device that stores electrical energy in the form of an electric field.
A. resistor,
B. capacitor,
C. transistor 2024

By what percentage does the capacitance change when the distance between the two parallel plates of a capacitor is doubled?
A. 25%,
B. 50%,
C. 75% 2024

What does changing the charge on a capacitor primarily affect?
A. Capacitance,
B. Voltage,
C. Resistance 2024

Insulating materials have the dielectric constant values of _______.
A. one,
B. greater than one,
C. less than one 2024

The equivalent capacitance _______ when the individual capacitors are connected in series.
A. increases,
B. decreases,
C. does not change 2024

Which capacitor is used as a a tuning capacitor?
A. Electrolytic capacitor,
B. Ceramic capacitor,
C. Variable capacitor 2024

Two conductors of capacitor of capacitance C are brought together to a third of their original separation. What is its capacitance now?
A. 1/9 C,
B. 1/3 C,
C. 3 C 2024

The capacitors are connected in series each capacitor has the same _______.
A. charge,
B. potential difference,
C. capacitance 2024

A capacitor consists of two _______ separated by a small distance.
A. conductors
B. insulators
C. semiconductors 2024

_______ use ceramic as the dielectric material.
A. Ceramic capacitors
B. Electrolytic capacitors
C. Supercapacitors 2024

When the distance between the two parallel plates of a capacitor is doubled, by what percent does its capacitance change?
A. decreases by 50%
B. increases by 50%
C. decreases by 25% 2025

The dielectric constant κ of insulating materials is _______.
A. greater than 1
B. less than 1,
C. equal to 0 2025

Which type of energy does the capacitor store?
A. kinetic
B. potential
C. both 2025

When the capacitors are connected in series, each capacitor has the same _______.
A. charge
B. capacitance
C. potential difference 2025

What happens to the capacitance of a variable capacitor when the distance between the plates in increased?
A. Capacitance increases
B. Capacitance decreases
C. Capacitance remains the same 2025

The dielectric constant κ of insulating materials is _______.
A. grater than 1
B. less than 1
C. equal to 0 2025

How does the capacitance of a capacitor vary when the charge on the capacitor is increased?
A. increases
B. remains constant
C. decreases 2025

The capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor increases when:
A. The distance between the plates increases
B. The area of the plates increases
C. The dielectric constant decreases 2025
a: The distance between the plates increases
The area of the plates increases

q:
In a combination of capacitors, the total capacitance is less than the smallest individual capacitance in:
A. Series combination
B. Parallel combination
C. Neither series nor parallel 2025

Film capacitors and ceramic capacitors are _______ capacitors.
A. simplest
B. polarized
C. non-polarized 2025

Variable capacitors mostly use _______ as the dielectric medium.
A. air
B. oxide layer
C. ceramic 2025

Which of the following capacitors are polarized capacitor?
A. electrolytic capacitors
B. ceramic capacitors
C. film capacitors 2025

What is the time constant τ of a capacitor charging or discharging process?
A. the product of the resistance and the capacitance
B. the sum of the resistance and the capacitance
C. the ration of the resistance to the capacitance 2025
a: the product of the resistance and the capacitance
the sum of the resistance and the capacitance
the ration of the resistance to the capacitance

q:
What capacitor is used as a tuning capacitor?
A. electrolytic capacitor
B. ceramic capacitor
C. variable capacitor 2025

Which capacitors are the ones that use the oxide layer as the dielectric material?
A. Film capacitors,
B. Ceramic capacitors,
C. Electrolytic capacitors 2025

When the distance between the two parallel plates of capacitor is doubled, by what percent does its capacitance change?
A. decreases 50 %
B. increases 50 %
C. decreases 0% 2025

_______ can also be used in a time-dependent circuit (timer circuit).
A. Capacitors
B. Resistors
C. Insulators 2025

A _______ capacitor is a non-polarized capacitor.
A. ceramic
B. variable
C. electrolytic 2025

_______ can also be used in a time-dependent circuit (timer circuit).
A. Inductors
B. Resistors
C. Capacitors 2026

The value of dielectric constant of vacuum is _______.
A. zero
B. one
C. two 2026

The product of the resistance and the _______ is called the time constant of a capacitor charging or discharging process.
A. capacitance
B. inductance
C. voltage 2026

When the distance between the two parallel plates of a capacitor is doubled, the capacitance becomes _______ of its initial value.
A. 20%
B. 50%
C. 80% 2026

The energy stored in a changed capacitor is given by _______
A. W = QV
B. W = 1/2 VQ
C. W = CV 2026
